paragraphs: I am applying for the [Role Title] position at [Company Name]. [Company Name] builds [what the company does — one sentence on the mission or product]. I built FIN — a financial management SaaS platform — for a closely related reason: small business owners in South Africa are routinely non-compliant with SARS not because they are careless, but because the tools that exist require accounting knowledge they do not have. I understand the problem of building software that matters to people who cannot afford for it to fail, and I want to bring that perspective to a team operating at [Company Name]'s scale.

Over the past two years I designed, built, and shipped FIN to production as a solo engineer: 620 000 lines of code, 331 REST endpoints across 30 Spring Boot controllers, a 62-table multi-tenant PostgreSQL schema, and 11 GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows covering build, test, SAST, secret scanning, and deployment to AWS. The stack is Java 17, Spring Boot 3.5, React 19, TypeScript, Docker, and AWS. I have built the payroll engine, the double-entry accounting engine, a spatial PDF parsing pipeline for South African bank statements, live PayFast subscription billing, and a full financial reporting suite. Every part of that system — schema design, service architecture, security, CI/CD, and production operations — I owned end to end.

I left a formal computer science programme at IU International University due to financial constraints and continued through self-directed study and production delivery. I completed the Google Cybersecurity Specialization and eleven Java and Spring Boot certifications through LinkedIn Learning, all while building FIN. The security knowledge from those certifications fed directly into production decisions: JWT authentication design, strict CSP configuration, rate limiting, and zero-credential infrastructure via AWS Secrets Manager. I am not a candidate who learned theory and is looking for a first project — I am a candidate who built a production system and formalised the theory in parallel.

The engineering culture I am looking for is one that values autonomy, continuous learning, and building for real impact. That is the environment I have already been operating in. I am ready to contribute to a distributed system at a scale I have not yet worked at, and I am the kind of engineer who gets there by reading, asking, shipping, and iterating. I would welcome the opportunity to speak with your team.
